name: Buttermilk Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease an 8-inch square pan with butter.
2. In a 3-quart saucepan, combine buttermilk and baking soda, then add corn syrup, butter, and sugar. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves and the mixture comes to a boil. Use a pastry brush dipped in hot water to remove sugar crystals from the sides of the pan.
3. Insert a candy thermometer into the mixture. Reduce the heat to low and continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches the soft-ball stage (238°F).
4. Remove the saucepan from heat and pour the mixture into a large, heat-proof mixing bowl. Let it cool to lukewarm. Stir in vanilla until the mixture thickens.
5. Add the chopped pecans and beat until well combined. Spread the fudge into the prepared pan. Use a knife to score the fudge into squares. Allow it to cool and firm up before refrigerating.
6. Cut the fudge into squares. This recipe makes about 1 pound of buttermilk fudge.
Note: The mixture will boil vigorously, so ensure you use a large 3-quart saucepan.
